Name: curvature_aware_mixed_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Mixed-Precision Routing for Efficient and Stable Training
Short Hypothesis: Develop a curvature-aware precision routing framework that dynamically adjusts the precision of network layers based on the Hessian trace and gradient covariance, leading to more efficient and stable training with minimal accuracy loss.
Related Work: Existing work in mixed-precision training, such as APTQ (Guan et al., 2024) and HMQAT (Huang et al., 2024), focuses on sensitivity metrics and bit reduction. However, they often lack a dynamic, curvature-aware approach to precision adjustment. Our proposal introduces a novel framework that integrates Hessian trace and gradient covariance to guide precision routing, ensuring both efficiency and stability.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware mixed-precision routing framework that dynamically adjusts the precision of neural network layers based on the Hessian trace and gradient covariance. This approach ensures efficient and stable training by optimizing resource allocation and maintaining model accuracy. Unlike existing methods that rely on static or heuristic-based precision adjustments, our framework leverages second-order information to make informed decisions, leading to reduced computational overhead and improved training dynamics. We validate our approach through empirical experiments on standard benchmarks, demonstrating significant improvements in training time and memory usage while maintaining or even improving model performance.

Experiments.Title: Hessian Trace and Gradient Covariance Analysis
Experiments.Description: Compute the Hessian trace and gradient covariance for each layer during training. This involves collecting and analyzing the second-order information to identify layers that are more sensitive to precision changes.
Experiments.Metrics: Hessian trace, gradient covariance, layer sensitivity score

Experiments.Title: Precision Routing Algorithm Development
Experiments.Description: Develop a precision routing algorithm that dynamically adjusts the precision of each layer based on the sensitivity score. The algorithm will use a threshold-based approach to switch between different precision levels (e.g., FP32, FP16, BF16).
Experiments.Metrics: Precision levels, computational overhead, memory usage

Experiments.Title: Training and Evaluation
Experiments.Description: Train models using the proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework on standard benchmarks such as CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet. Compare the performance in terms of training time, memory usage, and accuracy with baseline methods.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, accuracy, Top-1 and Top-5 accuracy for ImageNet

Experiments.Title: Ablation Study
Experiments.Description: Conduct an ablation study to evaluate the impact of different components of the precision routing framework, such as the Hessian trace and gradient covariance, on the overall performance. This will help isolate the contributions of each component.

Risk Factors and Limitations: Computational complexity of Hessian trace and gradient covariance calculations may introduce overhead, although this can be mitigated by using efficient approximations.
The dynamic nature of precision routing may introduce additional complexity in the training pipeline, requiring careful implementation and validation.
The effectiveness of the approach may vary across different architectures and datasets, necessitating extensive empirical evaluation.
